blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The locked door only eliminates one category of murder. Physical presence at the time of death. But whatever was done to Marcus — if something was done — didn't require being in the room with him when it happened. You do it before. He walks upstairs. He locks the door himself. And whatever is already inside him does the rest.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Something he consumed. Before he went up. The locked room isn't the alibi everyone thinks it is. It's actually evidence that the method was more deliberate than a crime of opportunity.
Davenport leaned back in his chair. Looked at the ceiling for a moment. Then back at Julian.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: If you're right, we're looking at premeditation.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Someone who planned this before they arrived at that house.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Someone who planned it carefully enough that a locked door would do their work for them afterward. Make everyone conclude it was natural. Make the investigation difficult to sustain without physical evidence.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Tox screen takes six weeks standard.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I know. A good defense attorney knows that too. Six weeks is a long time. Long enough for a case to go cold if it doesn't have other legs.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You think this person knows how investigations work.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I think this person has thought about every variable. Including that one.
